title: Long-Context Architectures
concept: Modern architectures (like FlashAttention) optimize memory to allow context windows of 100k+ tokens. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).'

options.text: The amount of text the AI can process in a single prompt.
The physical size of the computer screen.
How fast the AI can type its answers back to you.

exercises.explain: A context window determines how much text (tokens) an AI can read and think about at one time.

cards.title: Supercharging AI Memory
cards.body: Have you ever tried to read a whole textbook in one night? Your brain gets overwhelmed. Early AI models had the same problem: if you gave them a prompt longer than a few pages, they ran out of memory and forgot the beginning. Today, new math tricks let AIs read entire novels in a single prompt!

cards.title: What is a Long-Context Architecture?
cards.body: Long-context architectures are special designs, like FlashAttention, that change how an AI calculates attention. Instead of keeping a massive grid of all word-to-word relationships in expensive memory at once, they calculate it in smaller, smart chunks. This allows the AI to handle massive prompts of 100,000 words or more without crashing the computer.

cards.title: The Book Reader Analogy
cards.body: Imagine reading a massive fantasy book. Instead of turning the pages one by one, your brain has to remember every single word at the exact same time just to understand the plot. You would quickly get a headache! Traditional AI does this and runs out of 'brain space' (RAM). FlashAttention is like a speed-reader who highlights key points on the fly, remembering the story without needing to hold all 1,000 pages in active memory at once.

cards.title: Context Windows in Action
cards.body: Without a long-context setup, if you paste a 200-page financial report into an AI, the system will crash with an 'Out of Memory' error. With FlashAttention active, the AI easily digests the entire report in seconds and can instantly answer: 'On page 142, what was the profit margin?'

cards.title: Myth: Permanent Knowledge
cards.body: Many people think that 'long-context' means the AI has infinite, permanent memory. In reality, it only keeps this information in its head while you are actively chatting. Once you close the session, it forgets the details of that massive document!

cards.title: Memory Usage Comparison
cards.body: Compare how traditional AI handles memory versus how modern long-context architectures keep it efficient.

right.text: Splits the attention calculations into tiny, fast blocks to save GPU memory.

left.text: Calculates the entire massive attention grid at once, causing memory overload.

exercises.prompt: Why did older AI models crash when you gave them a prompt with thousands of words?

options.text: The AI gets bored and stops reading.
The GPU runs out of memory from storing giant tables of word connections.
The AI runs out of storage space on its hard drive.
The text contains words the AI has never seen before.

exercises.explain: Traditional attention math grows quadratically with prompt length, meaning double the text requires four times the memory. Long-context architectures fix this memory bottleneck.

exercises.prompt: True or False: When you upload a novel to a long-context AI, the AI permanently updates its core neural network weights with that novel's information.
exercises.explain: Even with a long context window of 100k+ tokens, the AI's training data remains unchanged. It is just reading the document temporarily in its active memory.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing term to complete the explanation.
exercises.sentence: To read huge documents, modern AI uses ___ to calculate attention in memory-efficient chunks.
exercises.explain: FlashAttention is a key memory-saving technique that makes long-context windows possible.

exercises.prompt: An AI is asked to explain how it handles a newly uploaded 300-page PDF. Which response is correct?

options.text: AI: 'I can analyze this entire 300-page book you just uploaded, but I will only remember it during our current conversation.'
AI: 'Since I read your 300-page book in this prompt, it is now permanently saved in my main brain for all other users to query.'
AI: 'I can read your 300-page book, but only if you translate it into binary code first.'

exercises.explain: AI Option B makes a common mistake: thinking that longer context means the AI actually learns and retains the information permanently. Option A shows the correct understanding of temporary, in-context processing.

exercises.prompt: How does FlashAttention help an AI model read a massive book without running out of memory?

options.text: It processes attention in smart, smaller chunks to save GPU memory.
It deletes random words from the user's prompt to save space.
It forces the AI to only read the first 100 words of a book.
It runs the entire AI model on a regular smartphone screen.

exercises.explain: FlashAttention and other long-context upgrades are game-changers, but sometimes we want to feed the AI files that are even larger than 100k tokens—like entire libraries of books! Next up: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).
